Petition Revoke Powers Of Trustee, Bring Trust Under Continuning Court Supervision, And Appoint General Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Apr-11-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition Revoke Powers Of Trustee, Bring Trust Under Continuning Court Supervision, And Appoint General Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Mar-24-2011 Continued To Apr-11-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-10-294020 (Conservatorship of Herbert H. Paas).

Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Lenore Gerard for Herbert H. Paas (Proposed Conservatee). Andrew Schultz for and with Andrea Leung (Temporary Trustee).

Mr. Schultz stated that they took their petition for conservatorship off calendar, that June Lewis filed a declination, that they still need a declination from the second nominee who is represented by Seth Skootsky, that he spoke with Mr. Skootsky who stated that his client will file the declination, and that he will probably need about a week to obtain the declination. In addition, Mr. Schultz requested that the Court keep the temp in place and extend the letters in the Trust matter. Ms. Gerard stated that Mr. Paas signed a power of attorney and an advanced health care directive naming Ms. Leung, that she would like to remain on to speak with Mr. Paas one more time to show him the documents, and that she would also like to speak one last time with the two parties who are declining to serve. The Court stated that it would continue the Trust matter to a Monday probate calendar and that once the declination was received that the matter would probably be pre-granted. The Court continued the Trust matter to April 11, 2011. Mr. Schultz is to prepare the order extending the temp in that matter. Mr. Schultz addressed the bond stating that everything should be combined in the Trust, that since there isn't a conservatorship proceeding that he did not include the 10% recovery amount, and that the bond should be set at $511,000.00. The Court directed Mr. Schultz to prepare a declaration regarding the bond and include everything he explained on the record today. Mr. Schultz' declaration is to be on file by close of business Wednesday, April 6, 2011.

Ms. Gerard is to remain on for the purposes requested above.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Mary Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
